title: Dynamic filtering and precision alteration of query responses responsive to request load
abstract: Embodiments relate to processing a request from a user device for access rights for a resource. An access management system can send a request to query a (e.g., cached or authoritative) data store for available access rights. The query may include an exact-match or fuzzy query. A set of access-right results responsive to the query can be identified. The system may transmit a communication to the user device that identifies the set, or a subset thereof. Upon receiving a selection of a result, the system can facilitate assigning access rights corresponding to the identified result to the user. In some instances, a level of precision at which a characteristic of an access-right result is identified and/or whether or how access rights are held depends on a request load.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An access management system for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources by facilitating query execution and filtering query results, the system comprising: a user-device interface configured to exchange communications with user devices; a data-store interface configured to exchange communications with each of one or more data store servers that manage one or more data stores; one or more data processors; and a non-transitory computer readable storage medium containing instructions which when executed on the one or more data processors, cause the one or more data processors to perform actions including: receiving, via the user-device interface, a first communication from a user device, the first communication corresponding to a request to be granted access to a resource, and the user device being remote from the access management system; identifying a request load for a resource, the request load corresponding to a quantity of requests that are requesting access to the resource be granted; identifying an initial access constraint that specifies a value or range of a characteristic of an access right or group of access rights, the initial access constraint being determined based on: input data in the first communication corresponding to input received via a user interface of the user device that identifies the constraint; or assignment data that identifies a characteristic of another access right or group of access rights for another resource and that were assigned to a user associated with the first communication; transmitting, via the data-store interface, a second communication to each of one or more data store servers that manage one or more data stores to identify which access rights amongst a plurality of access rights are available for the resource, each access right in the plurality of access rights being configured to permit a device or user assigned the access right to access to the resource, an access characteristic of the access permitted by each access right in the plurality of access rights being unique 

 across the plurality of access rights, and each of the one or more data store servers being remote from the access management system; receiving, via the data-store interface, a third communication from each of at least one of the one or more data store servers, the third communication identifying an availability of at least part of the set of access rights; determining a set of access-right results based on the response communication received from each of the at least one of the one or more data store servers, each access-right result complying with the initial constraint and each access- right result in the set of access-right results including an identification of one or more access rights in the set of access rights that were identified as being available; determining a level of precision for identifying a characteristic of access rights based on the request load; and transmitting, via the user-device interface, a fourth communication to the user device, the fourth communication identifying, for each of at least one access- right result in the set of access rights and at the level of precision, the characteristic of an access right identified in the access-right result. 2. The access management system for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources as recited in claim 1, wherein the actions further include: receiving, via the user-device interface, a fifth communication from the user device, the fifth communication including an identification of an access-right result in the at least one access-right result; and transmitting, via the data-store interface, a sixth communication that includes an instruction to identify one or more available access rights that are currently available for assignment and that correspond to the characteristic, at the level of precision, for the access right in the access-right result identified in the fifth communication. 3. The access management system for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources as recited in claim 2, wherein the at least one of the one or more data stores includes one or more cache data stores that includes cached indications indicating that access rights in the set of access rights are available for the resource, and wherein the sixth communication is transmitted to a data store server that manages an authoritative data store. 4. The access management system for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources as recited in claim 1, wherein the actions further include: 

 selecting an incomplete subset of the set of access-right results, the incomplete subset being selected by using a subset-selection technique, wherein the at least one access- right result is the incomplete subset of the set of access-right results, such that characteristics of access rights identified in other access-right results included in the set of access-right results but not in the subset are not identified in the fourth communication. 5. The access management system for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources as recited in claim 4, wherein the subset-selection technique includes pseudo-randomly selecting between at least some of the set of access-right results. 6. The access management system for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources as recited in claim 1, wherein the actions further include: transmitting a hold-instruction communication to the at least one of the one or more data store servers, the hold-instruction communication including an instruction to place, for each of the at least one access-right results, the one of more access rights in the access- right result on hold for a period of time so as to inhibit assigning any of the access rights during the period of time. 7. The access management system for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources as recited in claim 6, wherein the actions further include: determining, prior to the transmission of the hold-instruction communication and based on the request load, the period of time or that a hold is to be placed. 8. The access management system for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources as recited in claim 1 , wherein the characteristic is indicative of a location associated with at least one of the one or more access rights. 9. The access management system for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources as recited in claim 1 , wherein the one or more data stores include indications about which access rights are available via a primary channel and which access rights are available via a secondary channel. 10. The access management system for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources as recited in claim 1 , wherein identifying a request load includes predicting a request load. 11. A computer-implemented method for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources, the method comprising: receiving, via a user-device interface of an access management system, a first communication from a user device, the first communication corresponding to a request to be granted access to a resource, and the user device being remote from the access management system; identifying, at the access management system, a request load for a resource, the request load corresponding to a quantity of requests that are requesting access to the resource be granted; identifying, at the access management system, an initial access constraint that specifies a value or range of a characteristic of an access right or group of access rights, the initial access constraint being determined based on: input data in the first communication corresponding to input received via a user interface of the user device that identifies the constraint; or assignment data that identifies a characteristic of another access right or group of access rights for another resource and that were assigned to a user associated with the first communication; transmitting, via a data-store interface of the access management system, a second communication to each of one or more data store servers that manage one or more data stores to identify which access rights amongst a plurality of access rights are available for the resource, each access right in the plurality of access rights being configured to permit a device or user assigned the access right to access to the resource, an access characteristic of the access permitted by each access right in the plurality of access rights being unique across the plurality of access rights, and each of the one or more data store servers being remote from the access management system; receiving, via the data-store interface, a third communication from each of at least one of the one or more data store servers, the third communication identifying an availability of at least part of the set of access rights; determining, at the access management system, a set of access-right results based on the response communication received from each of the at least one of the one or more data store servers, each access-right result complying with the initial constraint and each access-right result in the set of access-right results including an identification of one or more access rights in the set of access rights that were identified as being available; 

 determining, at the access management system, a level of precision for identifying a characteristic of access rights based on the request load; and transmitting, via the user-device interface, a fourth communication to the user device, the fourth communication identifying, for each of at least one access-right result in the set of access rights and at the level of precision, the characteristic of an access right identified in the access-right result. 12. The computer-implemented method for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources as recited in claim 1 1, further comprising: receiving, via the user-device interface, a fifth communication from the user device, the fifth communication including an identification of an access-right result in the at least one access-right result; and transmitting, via the data-store interface, a sixth communication that includes an instruction to identify one or more available access rights that are currently available for assignment and that correspond to the characteristic, at the level of precision, for the access right in the access-right result identified in the fifth communication. 13. The computer-implemented method for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources as recited in claim 12, wherein the at least one of the one or more data stores includes one or more cache data stores that includes cached indications indicating that access rights in the set of access rights are available for the resource, and wherein the sixth communication is transmitted to a data store server that manages an authoritative data store. 14. The computer-implemented method for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources as recited in claim 1 1, further comprising: selecting an incomplete subset of the set of access-right results, the incomplete subset being selected by using a subset-selection technique, wherein the at least one access- right result is the incomplete subset of the set of access-right results, such that characteristics of access rights identified in other access-right results included in the set of access-right results but not in the subset are not identified in the fourth communication. 15. The computer-implemented method for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources as recited in claim 14, wherein the subset-selection technique includes pseudo-randomly selecting between at least some of the set of access-right results. 16. The computer-implemented method for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources as recited in claim 1 1, further comprising: transmitting a hold-instruction communication to the at least one of the one or more data store servers, the hold-instruction communication including an instruction to place, for each of the at least one access-right results, the one of more access rights in the access- right result on hold for a period of time so as to inhibit assigning any of the access rights during the period of time. 17. The computer-implemented method for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources as recited in claim 16, further comprising: determining, prior to the transmission of the hold-instruction communication and based on the request load, the period of time or that a hold is to be placed. 18. The computer-implemented method for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources as recited in claim 1 1 , wherein the characteristic is indicative of a location associated with at least one of the one or more access rights. 19. The computer-implemented method for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources as recited in claim 1 1 , wherein the one or more data stores include indications about which access rights are available via a primary channel and which access rights are available via a secondary channel. 20. The computer-implemented method for facilitating assignments of access rights for resources as recited in claim 11 , wherein identifying a request load includes predicting a request load.
